place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This is my first time at Ci Yuan Hawker Centre, and I visited on a Sunday evening at 8 pm, which is a public holiday. As a result, most stalls were closed or closing. Despite that, I was impressed by the variety of food options available here.

I look forward to returning another time to explore more!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: This is a newer hawker centre located on the ground floor of Ci Yuan Community Club. It’s cleaner & more organised than the older ones, with a good mix of food choices without too many repeats. Prices are still affordable & the overall atmosphere is relaxed - less crowded & not touristy.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I don’t live nearby so I hardly eat at Ci Yuan hawker center. Today I’m in the mood to eat some “unhealthy” kind of foods that I normally skip. So I had hokkien noodle (01-16), a duck drumstick (01-12) and fried cabbage (01-11).  Finish my lunch with chendol. I enjoyed myself. Please try out these food. My best experience so far eating at Ci Yuan hawker center. I find that the food is getting better over the years. Recommended 💕

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Newly open stall "文哥潮州鱼•蒸好吃 (no English name) yet.

Tried item C: Bitter Gourd with Egg soup ($4) + Dry Hor Fun ($1.50)
= $5.50 (Total) reasonable price.

Hor Fun (dry) with black sauce (mild sweetness), fried shallots, spring onions, mini pork lards (maybe chopped very small pc - you can ask for more if you like).
You can add more chilli paste or chopped chilli padi if you like more spicy kick 🔥 🥵  Shiok & Sweat! 💦

Bitter Gourd Soup MUST TRY!
Really generous ingredients like:
- thick & tender pork slices (maybe pork collar)
- minced pork (no fats surprisingly)
- pork balls (texture not hard when bite)
- brown strips (can someone enlighten me what it call?) thanks.
- bitter gourd not bitter (I'm a person who don't fancy bitter gourd, but it really hit my taste buds - good try for me!)

This soup really warms my heart and makes me feel missing my mum's hot soup...wow!

Met a friend there who also recommended me to try their Salted fish & peanuts pork patty & Vinegar Pork Trotters.

So I will come back again to try & share with you all again!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Clean, spacious and airy hawker center with a variety of food options. We had taliwang nasi lemak, chef wang fried rice, ccf and the sliced fish pao fan from a zi char stall.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Great pool with indoor and outdoor options. You could even swim when there is lightning alert. Indoor pool is cool and water is not salty. Toilet is a little run down but it’s ok.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
A nice beautiful place ... There is a library... Foods ... Places to sit n relax ...
Movie screen to watch movies... 😉
Bedok polyclinic...there is a library inside bedok hub...there is a foodplace to eat, swimming complex...

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: This is a large swimming complex and great for kids on the 5th floor. There are 3 pools. One for small kiddies, one intermediate and one advanced. Pool is not heated but good for warm weather days. The Olympic pool has a shelter. There is an activesg gym on the 6th floor that overlooks the pool if you want to work out (separate entrance fee of 2.50 sgd). However, the gym are strict with footwear and need to bring your own towel.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: So many activities here. U got library, gym, multi purpose hall, swimming pool, classes for booking. Amazing place

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Actually, It's a great location, close to the MRT and shops around. They Have  shopping stores and eateries as well.

It's a Fantastic place for kids to learn swimming at Level 5.They Have Indoor pools too in case it rains, So confirm wont miss the lessons.

Love the design of the place, very lively and green! Has a lot of activities happening as well related to community activities...🏊🤽🩱🌿🍃

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Pretty old compared to other bus interchange. But has decent food options. Bread shop, indian cuisine and snacks.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: A Malay male bus driver driving 107M to Hougang Interchange on 18/12/2025 (probably reach interchange at 11:20+pm) was very rude!  I may or may not have forgotten to tap my card when I board the bus, and after a few minutes away from the bus stop that I boarded he suddenly shouted at me angrily “EXCUSE ME, YOU GOT TAP YOUR CARD OR NOT??!!” And the bus was lil bit pack at that time, I don’t even dare to turn my head cuz ik I’ll be embarrassed from others passengers staring at me lol Why didn’t he NICELY tell me that I forgotten to tap my card when I boarded the bus?? And must wait till he drives halfway till the next bus stop? Like at least give me a “hello, I think you forgot to tap your card” instead of shouting right at me 🤮👎🏻👎🏻

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Their bus timings aren't accurate, much to people's inconvenience. Seems like Bus 329 departed by 7.16am, even though it was scheduled to come at 7.18am. Now I have to walk to the other end of this U-shaped interchange just to take 116.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Frustrated with the inconsistent timings for bus 102. I’m not sure if it’s due to a lack of buses, or if the scheduled bus driver is lazing off, but it’s not a good look when a bus is scheduled to depart at a certain time but doesn’t. I’ve experienced this on a few occasions and it’s really frustrating to see that the bus is supposed to depart once every 15 mins but in reality, skips a bus once in a while (leading to 30 mins intervals instead)

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Met an extremely helpful young man working at Tai Seng Mrt station by the name of Kai Yong. He spent a fair bit of time explaining to me how to get to my destination due to the underground works affecting the train direction at the moment. Super amazing service! I’m deeply impressed

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I want to compliment a crowd marshal by the name of Ricardo! He is a perfect example of excellent service where he rendered a great help to me just now by giving me an accurate and precise direction for me when I got lost in the station! He is very patient in his explanation and is always smiling even though i threw temple at him initially due to my flustered manner! He even helped me to carry my heavy luggage up the escalator as there isn't any lift at the exit.
Nevertheless I am very blessed to have met him and many thanks to him so that I can meet up with my family. Please help me to extend my thanks to him and I hope there are more such crowd marshals like him.
Thank you Ricardo and all at Tai Seng Mrt station!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
key amenities at taiseng station
1️⃣ lift located after exiting the gantry where it is exit A/C
2️⃣ toilet located after exiting the gantry where it is exit B
3️⃣ ATM (POSB/OCBC) located after exiting the gantry where it is exit C

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I was rushing for work and this morning encountered train delay and disruption. 3 trains in a row people were asked to alight at Tai Seng and wait for the next train. This caused delays to my journey and disappointment with the service.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Worst train station to be at during peak hours. At around 6-640pm, you can’t even get on the train, especially towards Bishan. Be prepared to wait for at least 3-4 trains during that time. People will just push their way in just to board the train.
Beware. But some hack for anyone who read this, there will be an empty train at 621pm. You can board that train to avoid the squeezing.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: This was renovated recently and there is now a childcare to help cater to the demand in the area. There is an eldercare centre on the fourth level to cater to our rapidly ageing population. There is also a sheltered basketball court so that hoopers can hoop even if it is raining.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
We're here for vaccination for kids.

They are very systematic.

Everything prepared,  staffs are alert.
Thank u for it.

The place is wide and open.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Tiled badminton courts

What I like:
- Air-conditioning
- Ample of rest space
- walking distance from bus stop
- convenience store beside the court

What I don't like:
- There is a big fan in the middle of the hall, can't lift shots too high when playing badminton
- the net stand is too wide, resulting in 1 court having to offset the net position awkwardly

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Went for vaccination here. It was a good process, quick and all in order. Took about 40 minutes for the queue and the waiting. The place not that huge for sport it seems. Just one basketball field inside.

There is a small store inside fully unmanned with gate. Will try next time again when visiting here.

The carpark was closed that day. I don't know how big or if it is fully functional carpark. Need to park at the hdb carpark before reaching or the one after which longer walk.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:

I had to ask the staff who were very helpful.
Why not post signs clearly to help ppl along.

review_text: The park itself is fine if you don't intend to go onto the other side of the waterway because the number of bridges linking the two sides are few and far apart. I am not sure why it is designed like that. We happened to be on the opposite side of the "main attractions" like Waterway Point, SAFRA, etc and we just could not get over easily. I guess if you are aiming to have lots of steps, this is the place to be.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Great place for a family walk. Quite a lot of wildlife and nature you can spot here if you look closely.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Nature's serenity and wonder, 😜 if you don't mind just a quiet morning walk or family walk ☺️🍀😎💕👍🏽🌈

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This is my 3rd time here. Although I stay in Jurong West. Took over an hour travel I still miss this place. I hike from Punggol MRT to Lorong Halus Wetland Centre.  On the way we by passed lot of nice place like One Punggol we ate at hawker Center and proceed to waterway park. Along the way u can see few bridge it connecting both side. Easy walk path.
Please see photo enclosed.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: A peaceful escape from the city! Perfect for a scenic jog, bike ride, or just a relaxing stroll by the water.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Quiet, underrated gem in the northeast. Punggol Park has a really chill vibe, especially around the big lake in the middle. Great for evening walks, light jogs, or just sitting down to unwind after a long day.

Nice mix of facilities too. There’s a Tenderbest nearby for easy bites, a gym pod if you want a quick workout, plus water coolers and vending machines around for convenience. You’ll also spot exercise stations catered for the elderly, which is a thoughtful touch. There is a nice woodball corner as well and also an open area for football.

Plenty of shaded paths and open spaces, so it never feels too crowded. You’ll see families, cyclists, and even people fishing by the pond. Simple, not overly fancy, but that’s part of the charm.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
If u are in Hougang / Sengkang area, and wanted a park to stroll or exercise.  Try this Punggol Park.
Pls note that although it called Punggol Park, it’s actually in end of upper Serangoon, Hougang area.
It’s a smaller to Bedok Reservior Park, but it’s wide enough for most outdoor exercise.
Peaceful and tranquil.  U can have a drink/ food at the eatery: Tenderbest.  They have both indoor AC and alfresco dining area.

Also, if u want to walk/ cycle more, it’s just 5mins walk away to the Serangoon Park Connector.  Which will lead u to Kallang southwards or Northwards will lead u to Punggol Point or Changi Pt via Pasir Ris PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Actually, It's a Nice and quiet park with a running loop around a pond.The park with a lot of place to rest chair benches. It's shady with huge trees and bird chirping. The place is Great for walking, jogging or biking. You can come here anytime u want some peace and to de- stress through a nice and scenic walk in the park. At Night time the lights around the pond makes for a cool background. Overall a good place to bring the family and have some exercise. 🌳🌱🌿🍂🍃🍀🪺🌸💮🐦🐦‍⬛🚴🏃🚶🧎🤸

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: attended a 3 day wake at the oratory of the Church of Nativity recently. On one of the evenings, I went for a walk in the park. It was pleasant to be in nature, to reflect on the life. The park was however crowded in the evening with people exercising, cycling. walking their dogs and having picnics. Love the trees.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
It's a mind-blowing park for spending time with nature ,any time can go and spend time in peace , best place for morning time exercises and walking or running , meditation etc etc . Early in the morning the feeling here is very nice and quiet , the public toilet is also here ,
One wedding machine is also there to take your favourite drinks .

You’ll also spot exercise stations catered for the elderly, which is a thoughtful touch. There is a nice woodball corner as well and also an open area for football.

Plenty of shaded paths and open spaces, so it never feels too crowded. You’ll see families, cyclists, and even people fishing by the pond. Simple, not overly fancy, but that’s part of the charm.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
If u are in Hougang / Sengkang area, and wanted a park to stroll or exercise.  Try this Punggol Park.
Pls note that although it called Punggol Park, it’s actually in end of upper Serangoon, Hougang area.
It’s a smaller to Bedok Reservior Park, but it’s wide enough for most outdoor exercise.
Peaceful and tranquil.  U can have a drink/ food at the eatery: Tenderbest.  They have both indoor AC and alfresco dining area.

Also, if u want to walk/ cycle more, it’s just 5mins walk away to the Serangoon Park Connector.  Which will lead u to Kallang southwards or Northwards will lead u to Punggol Point or Changi Pt via Pasir Ris PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Actually, It's a Nice and quiet park with a running loop around a pond.The park with a lot of place to rest chair benches. It's shady with huge trees and bird chirping. The place is Great for walking, jogging or biking. You can come here anytime u want some peace and to de- stress through a nice and scenic walk in the park. At Night time the lights around the pond makes for a cool background. Overall a good place to bring the family and have some exercise. 🌳🌱🌿🍂🍃🍀🪺🌸💮🐦🐦‍⬛🚴🏃🚶🧎🤸

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: attended a 3 day wake at the oratory of the Church of Nativity recently. On one of the evenings, I went for a walk in the park. It was pleasant to be in nature, to reflect on the life. The park was however crowded in the evening with people exercising, cycling. walking their dogs and having picnics. Love the trees.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
It's a mind-blowing park for spending time with nature ,any time can go and spend time in peace , best place for morning time exercises and walking or running , meditation etc etc . Early in the morning the feeling here is very nice and quiet , the public toilet is also here ,
One wedding machine is also there to take your favourite drinks .

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
It's an MRT station that works for the surrounding neighborhood. It's clean, well structured, and connected to Heartland Mall. Is it any unique, no. Does it work as a public transportation facility, yes. If you're coming through Kovan or Hougang, you'll likely pass through this.

Just be mindful of which exit you're heading towards, or else you may be walking around blindly and end up walking a fair bit further.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Connected to the North East Line. Heartland Mall is located beside the MRT Station.

The Kovan Market and Food Centre is also within walking distance from the Mrt Station. Nice local food can be found there.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: One of the stations along the North East (purple) line which connects to a shopping centre (Heartland Mall) and Hawker Centre. There’s enough ancillary services within the basement - $10 haircut (Okinawa), OCBC and UOB ATMs, convenience shop (Buzz). Control station is always well-staffed with friendly and helpful captains. Handicap friendly and well-air conditioned too. Toilets are available at the ground level with taxi stands and passenger drop off areas on both sides of the exits - one just outside Kovan Residences, and the other outside Heartland Mall. Very convenient overall - both drop off areas and taxi stands/bus stops are sheltered across too. You can get almost everything you need here at Kovan - shopping, food, electronics, tuition centers, entertainment (bowling), fitness (anytime fitness), clinics, spas and much more. Many famous eateries around as well - Lola Cafe, Punggol Nasi Lemak, Yaowarat, Chicken Pot, etc. You will not run out of options for sure.

review_text:

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Visited this community club for a swim on a Tuesday and it was a really pleasant experience. The crowd was minimal, which made the whole session feel relaxed and unhurried. There are two pools available—one indoor and one outdoor—so you can choose depending on your preference or weather. There’s also a feature pool that has just been renovated and recently reopened, and it looks clean and refreshed. Overall, it’s a nice, well-maintained spot for a quiet swim without the usual crowd. There are lifeguards on duty to keep us safe!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
This swimming complex have slides and water features
2 large full length swimming pool. One of the pool is under shelter.
Locker is available for valuable item storage.
Parking is available for car, motocycle,  Bicycle.
Next to food court and there are food available together at the sport complex.
There will be swimming classes and may be crowded some of the time.
Limited table and chair around the pool while there is a stand next to the open pool.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
This swimming pool finally reopened after more than 3 years of closure.

It now has 5 types of slide rides, with Rainbow dash being the only multi-slide ride. They are all MUCH milder than the ones in Wild Wild Wet.

The lifeguards are pretty strict about height limits:

Purple slide (Cotton Candy Bowl) - 1.3m height limit

orange slide (Sugar Rush) - 1.3m height limit

Green slide (Jelly Lime Loops) - 1.2m height limit

Pink slide (Jellybears Swiggle) - 1.2m height limit

The lifeguards are also very particular about wearing of "hard objects", even watches by adults/parents. Yet, ironically, I see authorized influencers being allowed to wear GoPro cameras!!

There are mini paddle boats for children 4yo and above, with minimum weight limit 30 kg, and requires adult supervision at all times.

Water guns are NOT Allowed !!

Toilets are clean.

There's no more food eatery at the balcony area near the entrance.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
The swimming complex is quite big and has 4 adult water slides. Note that jewellery such as earrings or necklaces are not allowed, and swimsuits with zippers are prohibited. Kids need to be at least 120–130 cm tall to use the slides.

It gets very crowded on weekends, especially at the kids’ water play area. The adult slides were fine, just some queuing, especially for the longer slide. The Sugar Rush slide was really fast and fun.

The biggest downside is the changing/shower rooms. Around 5:30 PM, the queue gets extremely long, so it’s better to go earlier if you want to avoid the wait.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: It was a super bad experience at the sliding pool. My two girls was not allowed to take the slide just because their swimming wear got a zipper at the front. Going down slides shld be by sitting and sliding down. How in the world the front zipper affect the slide down process. Really disappointed with the system enforced. Really disappoint the kids on their school holiday.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Arty Learning @ Hougang | Enrichment Classes for Kids
review_text:
Just one month into Artylearning, and we are already seeing encouraging progress. The recommended bridging classes have been very helpful in supporting a child who was initially confused with alphabet recognition, reading, and phonics sounds.

We are truly grateful for the patient guidance and interactive activities that continue to spark interest and build confidence in learning. The session videos and feedback shared after every class also help parents better understand the child’s progress and learning journey.

Thank you to the teachers for making learning engaging and supportive!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Arty Learning @ Hougang | Enrichment Classes for Kids
review_text:
Dear Arty Learning School Team,

​We are writing to express our sincere gratitude and to highly commend Teacher Evf for her exceptional dedication and impact on our son.
​He is learning so much and truly thrives under her guidance. He comes home every day talking about how much fun he has at school and eagerly shares everything he learned.

​We especially appreciate Teacher Evf’s patience and creativity. For instance, when he needs a little extra time to focus on his writing, instead of showing frustration, she turns it into a fun game by saying "Wakey wakey!" This brilliant approach keeps him incredibly engaged and excited to learn rather than discouraged.

​Because of the safe and wonderful environment you provide and the fantastic impact Teacher Evf has had on his educational journey, we would absolutely love for him to continue learning under her in the future.

​Thank you, Teacher Evf, and thank you, Arty Learning School and all the staff for making the classroom such a joyful, supportive, and creative place!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Arty Learning @ Hougang | Enrichment Classes for Kids
review_text: My daughter Hazel started her journey with Arty at the age of 4 year old. She enjoyed every lesson. We like that they incorporate fun play sessions to have the kids engage in the lessons. I can’t see why any child will dislike attending classes here. My daughter is 6 this year, and she just graduated from Arty in March (year 2026) . I am happy that she now enjoy reading books to us and actively trying to pronounce any words she encounter. I can’t wait to send my younger daughter there to learn when she turns older. Arty Learning @ Hougang.. you are truly amazing :)

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Arty Learning @ Hougang | Enrichment Classes for Kids
review_text: Both my kids have been with Arty Learning since they were 4 years old, and I couldn’t be more grateful for the wonderful learning experience they’ve had. My children have become confident readers, and it truly shows. My elder child, who is now in Primary 1, transitioned to formal schooling with ease. He has had no struggles with reading, spelling, or understanding his worksheets and instructions. This strong foundation in literacy and comprehension was built through the years at Arty Learning. I would highly recommend Arty Learning to any parent who wants to give their child a confident start in their learning journey! Special thanks to Teacher Naz and Teacher Joyce for their dedication and care in my children's learning journey!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Arty Learning @ Hougang | Enrichment Classes for Kids
review_text: Visited this language centre to enrol my 6-year-old for beginner Mandarin classes. The classroom was tiny, barely fitting 3-4 students, and the noise from the neighbouring unit was so distracting. No proper soundproofing. It felt more like a playground than a proper learning environment. The chairs were uncomfortable, the space was cramped, and there was no dedicated waiting area for parents. My kid didn’t enjoy it and kept saying it was “too loud” and “boring.” Not suitable for young learners at all. Definitely not worth the fees they’re charging.

place_name: Arty Learning @ Hougang | Enrichment Classes for Kids
review_text:
Just one month into Artylearning, and we are already seeing encouraging progress. The recommended bridging classes have been very helpful in supporting a child who was initially confused with alphabet recognition, reading, and phonics sounds.

We are truly grateful for the patient guidance and interactive activities that continue to spark interest and build confidence in learning. The session videos and feedback shared after every class also help parents better understand the child’s progress and learning journey.

Thank you to the teachers for making learning engaging and supportive!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Arty Learning @ Hougang | Enrichment Classes for Kids
review_text:
Dear Arty Learning School Team,

​We are writing to express our sincere gratitude and to highly commend Teacher Evf for her exceptional dedication and impact on our son.
​He is learning so much and truly thrives under her guidance. He comes home every day talking about how much fun he has at school and eagerly shares everything he learned.

​We especially appreciate Teacher Evf’s patience and creativity. For instance, when he needs a little extra time to focus on his writing, instead of showing frustration, she turns it into a fun game by saying "Wakey wakey!" This brilliant approach keeps him incredibly engaged and excited to learn rather than discouraged.

​Because of the safe and wonderful environment you provide and the fantastic impact Teacher Evf has had on his educational journey, we would absolutely love for him to continue learning under her in the future.

​Thank you, Teacher Evf, and thank you, Arty Learning School and all the staff for making the classroom such a joyful, supportive, and creative place!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Arty Learning @ Hougang | Enrichment Classes for Kids
review_text: My daughter Hazel started her journey with Arty at the age of 4 year old. She enjoyed every lesson. We like that they incorporate fun play sessions to have the kids engage in the lessons. I can’t see why any child will dislike attending classes here. My daughter is 6 this year, and she just graduated from Arty in March (year 2026) . I am happy that she now enjoy reading books to us and actively trying to pronounce any words she encounter. I can’t wait to send my younger daughter there to learn when she turns older. Arty Learning @ Hougang.. you are truly amazing :)

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Arty Learning @ Hougang | Enrichment Classes for Kids
review_text: Both my kids have been with Arty Learning since they were 4 years old, and I couldn’t be more grateful for the wonderful learning experience they’ve had. My children have become confident readers, and it truly shows. My elder child, who is now in Primary 1, transitioned to formal schooling with ease. He has had no struggles with reading, spelling, or understanding his worksheets and instructions. This strong foundation in literacy and comprehension was built through the years at Arty Learning. I would highly recommend Arty Learning to any parent who wants to give their child a confident start in their learning journey! Special thanks to Teacher Naz and Teacher Joyce for their dedication and care in my children's learning journey!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Arty Learning @ Hougang | Enrichment Classes for Kids
review_text: Visited this language centre to enrol my 6-year-old for beginner Mandarin classes. The classroom was tiny, barely fitting 3-4 students, and the noise from the neighbouring unit was so distracting. No proper soundproofing. It felt more like a playground than a proper learning environment. The chairs were uncomfortable, the space was cramped, and there was no dedicated waiting area for parents. My kid didn’t enjoy it and kept saying it was “too loud” and “boring.” Not suitable for young learners at all. Definitely not worth the fees they’re charging.

review_text: SBS is soooo good at managing the crowd. Peak hour 8:45am on weekday, only 1 lane inwards and 5 lanes outwards. Handicap is also used by aunty exiting. 1 vs 6 lanes. How? Everyone late for work altogether, such a perfect transport service.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Clean, well maintained. There are places to sit, lift and wheelchair accessibility.
This is for purple line, the last station is for punggol, and other side is harbourfront.
The last train is arround 12:30.
Exit b is for mall and hougang central and a exit can take you to bus interchange.
Train frequency is 5 min.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We are truly impressed with the Chinese New Year celebration organised for the children at ELFA Hougang. The teachers’ performance of the lion dance was filled with enthusiasm and joy, creating an engaging and festive atmosphere for everyone. It was heartwarming to see the children joining in with shouts of excitement and dancing along, fully immersed in the celebration.

The art and craft activity with calligraphy was another highlight, allowing the children to have even more fun while exploring their creativity and connecting with the traditions of the celebration.

Overall, it was a wonderful experience for both the children and parents. Thank you to the school and teachers for making this celebration so memorable and enjoyable! I love ELFAAAA

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: We’ve had such a wonderful experience with this preschool. The teachers are warm, nurturing, and genuinely care about the children’s wellbeing. My child has grown so much in confidence and independence, and always comes home excited to share what they did in school. We truly feel supported as partners in our child’s development. Highly recommend this preschool to any parent looking for a caring and engaging early learning environment.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We are glad to engage my daughter first learning journey with Elfa. Wide range of enrichment activities, opportunities and exposure for the child to explore and pick up soft skills on top of curriculum schedules.

Annual year end concert is a heart felt appreciation to testify their growing up journey. Thank you Elfa for walking with us.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Awesome performance put up by the children, made possible with the guidance and patience of ELFA educators! It’s really heartening to see the growth in the children and more so when they genuinely enjoy putting up the concert! Thank you for the effort, Teacher Pat and all the teachers of ELFA!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Elfa preschool provides the children an enriching experiences to explore themselves through interactive learning. The enrichment classes like abacus and speech and drama also provides the children to prepare them to speak up confidently and challenge them mentally.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Had tried 88 prawn noodle at #01-56

Old school taste. Dried noodle is tasty, very spicy but shiok. Remember to ask for less spicy if you are not so hot.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: 25 years never come this hawker centre. Heard  just renovation. Impressed with the food choices. So many good Chinese and Malay food. Wanton mee, kolo mee, yong tao fu, chicken rice, tutu kueh. Clean and well ventilated. Parking maybe hard but can park at kinetic mall and walk over.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I discovered this new stall after the recent renovation of this very popular food centre. The steam fish extremely fresh and deliciously prepared. One of the best I have eaten which is so value-for-money for a healthier meal in food centre.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There are plenty of food options available, from malay to chinese and many others, making it a great spot to explore different flavors in one place. I also tried the famous putu piring, which was soft, a little bit sweet, and fragrant. The place can get quite busy but still worth to try.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Came here craving teh tarik, satay, and tu tu kueh — and all three wishes came true!

☕ Teh tarik was fragrant, smooth, and nicely pulled.
🍢 Satay was juicy, well-marinated, and smoky with delicious peanut sauce.
🍰 Tu tu kueh was soft, warm, and freshly made — comforting and nostalgic.

Perfect place if you want a mix of classic local flavours in one visit.
Will definitely come back again 👍

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My daughter attended Tr Xiao’s P6 Science class. Mr Xiao drilled the use of keywords and conceptual understanding across various science topics in a few months.

Her results soared from AL7 from P5 to AL3 in prelims. Her confidence was at a high by the end of prelims. She also mentioned her classmates in Mr Xiao’s class mostly scored between AL1 to AL3 by prelims. It reassured me that she’s in good hands for her PSLE prep.

My girl scored her best results of AL2 in PSLE. We are grateful to have found Tr Xiao and his team of supportive teachers. I highly recommend Edify for those who are looking for tuition class.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: My son joined Edify’s teacher Ko weekly science lessons for P6 (2025) and attained AL2 for his PSLE. Teacher Ko is an excellent teacher who teaches science concepts very well and he is always happy to answer any questions whenever in doubt. Also big thanks to Teacher Xiao for his weekly sessions on MCQ online. They are excellent educators who are clear of the concepts and will explain them in details. I have also enrolled my younger son to Edify. Thank you very much.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: It was a rainy day, and the traffic lights on the opposite side were not working. We called the admin, and they were very kind and helped bring my kids safely to their tuition centre. I was truly touched by their care and kindness towards the students. Thank you, Edify.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We've had a very positive experience with Edify Kovan.  The teachers are passionate, approachable and care about each student's progress.
My 2 daughters have shown great improvement in their academics.
The facilities are clean, safe and well maintained.
Overall, we are very happy with our choice and would highly recommend other.
Thanks Teacher Ko, Teacher Shaun and Teacher John

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
The teachers here are familiar with the syllabus because they were ex-MOE teachers. This gives us a peace of mind knowing that the teachers know what they are talking about. Why pay for inexperienced tutors who charge an arm and a leg.

They are also dedicated and encouraging, often going the extra mile to help students understand the concepts.

The PSLE journey can be tiring and frustrating but the teachers at Edify, in particular Tr Ko and Tr Shaun, have made it more bearable.

Thank you for the ride, Edify.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Dear Management Team,
I am writing to share a truly exceptional experience I recently had at your store.
While shopping for meat at GIANT Food, I was warmly welcomed by an outstanding employee whose name tag identified him as Mr. Youn Sang. From the moment I approached the counter, he made my entire experience feel special and valued.
What impressed me most was his attentiveness to everyone around him. No customer in line was left unattended. Mothers with children were acknowledged with patience and kindness, and every single person received a warm, personalized greeting. His professionalism, positive energy, and genuine care for customers truly stood out.
It is rare to encounter someone who takes such pride in delivering exceptional service. Mr. Youn Sang made my shopping experience not only pleasant but memorable, and I felt it was important that your management team be made aware of the remarkable job he is doing.
Please extend my sincere appreciation to him. Employees like Mr. Youn Sang are a tremendous asset to your organization.
Sincerely,
Jay.C

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Worst hypermarket experience. Only three checkout counters were open on a Saturday night, which naturally led to long queues. We waited about 40 minutes just to check out. If the store is short-staffed, it should reconsider operating 24 hours. Giant, please learn from Sheng Siong.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This is one of the best mega warehouses in Singapore to shop on the East side as it carries many brands of food, household n electrical items . The layout is good where one can shop in comfort. There is a food court that has a nice range of food where one can indulge after the shopping. A great place to stock up essentials for home as the ample parking is convenient to trolley ones purchase to the car. One can find wonderful bargains as the store holds promotional product brands that are a steal. Happy shopping , happy family.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Today, January 12, 2026, at the Giant food court, I ordered *mee hoon* soto from the Nasi Padang stall, asking for an extra chicken add-on for $2.00. After I collected the food, it was not hot and contained only about one tablespoon of chicken. I asked the lady, who sounded Indonesian, about it. She was not happy when I asked her if the $2.00 chicken had been added. She pulled the bowl, and not wanting to create a scene, I just pulled it back and left. I'm very disappointed with her attitude; at the very least, she could have explained nicely. This will be the last time.

review_text:
Final Giant Hypemarket at Tampines ✔️✔️✔️✔️✔️👍👍👍

Giant Hypermarket at Tampines is ideal for one-stop grocery needs if you live in this area. There is a wide range of electronics, kitchenware, groceries, a semi-wet market, fruits, and vegetables. The prices are reasonable. There are also food stalls serving plenty of local dishes. Halal and vegetarian options are available and typically served until around 9:30 PM. Additionally, there is ample parking space with free parking available. If you live in this area, it’s an excellent place to shop for your daily needs.
Chris
17 Sept 2025 11am

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Dear Management Team,
I am writing to share a truly exceptional experience I recently had at your store.
While shopping for meat at GIANT Food, I was warmly welcomed by an outstanding employee whose name tag identified him as Mr. Youn Sang. From the moment I approached the counter, he made my entire experience feel special and valued.
What impressed me most was his attentiveness to everyone around him. No customer in line was left unattended. Mothers with children were acknowledged with patience and kindness, and every single person received a warm, personalized greeting. His professionalism, positive energy, and genuine care for customers truly stood out.
It is rare to encounter someone who takes such pride in delivering exceptional service. Mr. Youn Sang made my shopping experience not only pleasant but memorable, and I felt it was important that your management team be made aware of the remarkable job he is doing.
Please extend my sincere appreciation to him. Employees like Mr. Youn Sang are a tremendous asset to your organization.
Sincerely,
Jay.C

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Worst hypermarket experience. Only three checkout counters were open on a Saturday night, which naturally led to long queues. We waited about 40 minutes just to check out. If the store is short-staffed, it should reconsider operating 24 hours. Giant, please learn from Sheng Siong.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This is one of the best mega warehouses in Singapore to shop on the East side as it carries many brands of food, household n electrical items . The layout is good where one can shop in comfort. There is a food court that has a nice range of food where one can indulge after the shopping. A great place to stock up essentials for home as the ample parking is convenient to trolley ones purchase to the car. One can find wonderful bargains as the store holds promotional product brands that are a steal. Happy shopping , happy family.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Today, January 12, 2026, at the Giant food court, I ordered *mee hoon* soto from the Nasi Padang stall, asking for an extra chicken add-on for $2.00. After I collected the food, it was not hot and contained only about one tablespoon of chicken. I asked the lady, who sounded Indonesian, about it. She was not happy when I asked her if the $2.00 chicken had been added. She pulled the bowl, and not wanting to create a scene, I just pulled it back and left. I'm very disappointed with her attitude; at the very least, she could have explained nicely. This will be the last time.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
✔️ Review of Hougang Mall
90 Hougang Ave 10, Singapore
538766

💙💙💙 I'm excited to share that I was selected as the winner of the Water Wally & Sally Plushie giveaway for Singapore's World Water Day 2026! To claim my prize, I visited Hougang Mall, which gave me the perfect opportunity to revisit and explore the area, bringing back cherished childhood memories.

Having recently moved to Tampines, I traveled to Hougang Mall, which took about an hour. The trip was a nostalgic journey back to my younger days when I often shopped there.

Hougang Mall provides a wide selection of stores that cater to the needs of most local residents.

It houses numerous shops and recently opened Food & Beverage outlets, such as NTUC FairPrice, BreakTalk, a food court, various restaurants, pharmacies, an electronics retailer (Harvey Norman), jewelry stores, beauty salons, spas, hair salons, and apparel shops.

The mall tends to be busy daily and can become quite crowded during weekends. It also regularly hosts events and promotional activities.

With its inviting ambiance and friendly staff, shopping at Hougang Mall offers a pleasant experience.

It is a must-visit destination for those nearby seeking convenient and diverse shopping options—a great place to unwind and refresh.

Visit Hougang Mall, a true Heartland Mall, and enjoy a fun overall experience.

🙂 All essential items can be found here without the need to travel to the city.

Chris
17 April 2026

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Nice neighborhood mall with almost everything you need. Food options are plenty and the prices are quite reasonable, which is always a big plus.

The air conditioning is also very good. Very important, because shopping without good AC is basically cardio.

Only downside is that it does not connect directly to the MRT station. That would make it much more convenient.

Overall, a good mall for daily needs, food and a comfortable break from the heat.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Newly Upgraded Hougang Mall with many New F&B Outlets. Spoilt for choice, variety Authentic European & SEA Food Stalls here. Vibrant & POSITIVE ENERGY ENVIRONMENT Shopping Mall here. Can't wait for the New Renovated Hougang Library at Level 4 coming June/July...hopefully.
There is Value Shop & other Reasonable priced groceries & snacks.
Entertainment Game Station, NTUC Fair Price, BreakTalk, Wok Hey, Pet Lovers, Pharmacies, Jewellery shop, Beauty, Facial, Nail polish, Spa & Hair Salon.
Harvey Norman, Data Shoes & other brands. Enjoy your One Stop North East Hougang Central HG Shopping Mall here.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Hougang Mall is a vibrant shopping destination in Singapore, offering a mix of local and international brands. The mall's cozy atmosphere and friendly staff make shopping a pleasant experience. With various dining options, including popular food courts and cafes, it's a great place to relax and refuel. The mall also hosts regular events and promotions, adding to its appeal. While it can get crowded during peak hours, the overall experience is enjoyable. A must-visit for anyone in the area looking for a convenient and diverse shopping experience

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The new renovated mall definitely gives it a fresh look! However, I don't understand why they shut down the escalator from level 1 to level 3. I see elderly folks struggling to climb up and down. For those of us who can walk, it's not a big deal. I see people who are able-bodied waiting for the already small lift, while people who really need it are waiting too.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Food was absolutely Nom Nom! This is the 2nd time I’ve been to Lola’s (1st was around 3 years ago), and I’m glad to say that the quality and service is still top notch!  The hojicha oat latte is a sure must get cuz it’s just that good hahahah. For the sides, Brussels sprouts were well seasoned, and chicken wings were crispy and meaty. For the mains, the pasta was al dente and flavourful, and the salmon was really nice as well. The pistachio strawberry tart was super yummy as well!!! It was quite busy when we went but the food was pumped out of the kitchen surprisingly fast, around 5-10mins after ordering, everything came.  10/10 will recommend coming back to try new things!!! Be sure to get a reservation cuz walk ins had to wait for quite awhile!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We had heard that Lola serves pretty good food, and we've passed by several times, always seeing the place packed.

This time, we visited at around 1.45pm on a weekday, but it was still a full house. We took a queue number, with two groups ahead of us, and waited about 30 minutes before being seated.

Overall, the food is decent by Singapore café standards. Considering the quality, taste, and portion sizes, it's reasonably priced and offers good value for a café in Singapore.

The desserts were nice too, especially the tiramisu.

Some feedback on the food: the salmon was quite fishy (I'm a salmon lover, and I found it fishy too). The Brussels sprouts were rather oily and over-fried, with the inner layers also being fried, leaving them bitter and greasy.

As for the Strawberry Pistachio Tart, the combination felt a little off. The strawberry and pistachio didn't complement or enhance each other's flavors. Instead, when eaten together, it had a strange aftertaste that reminded us of plastic or an electrical taste—we couldn't really describe it, but it felt quite off.

Other than that, the overall food, dining experience, and environment were good. I would recommend visiting during the non-peak period between 3pm and 5pm. Outside of those hours, the restaurant is usually very busy.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Had a truly delightful dining experience at Lola’s Restaurant @ Simon Road ❤️

From the moment we stepped in, the warm ambience and cozy atmosphere made it such a relaxing place for family gatherings and casual catch-ups. The service crew were attentive, friendly, and made us feel very welcomed throughout the evening.

The food was beautifully presented and packed with flavour. The seafood dishes were fresh and satisfying, especially the creamy prawn and mussel combination paired with toasted bread — rich, comforting, and perfect for sharing. Their pasta dishes were equally impressive, with generous portions and bold flavours that kept everyone wanting more.

Not to be missed were the desserts 🍰✨
The waffle dessert with ice cream and cheesecake bites was indulgent yet balanced, making it a sweet ending to a wonderful meal.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
20th Apr 2026.
Brunch at 1030hrs, soon after opening, there are already diners.
Ordered the pulled pork with an extra avocado & miso salmon.
The pork is shredded, easy to chew. Missy had the salmon.
Meal was washed down with Hojicha latte, same for missy with Chai latte. Quite a small cup, but enough.
Signed up for member which came with a foc cake & 10% off the bill on birthday month. Very nice touch.
The young staffs are customer friendly, even offered to take photos for us.
Nice place.... Quiet ambience except for the two ladies who chatted nonstop at a volume louder than the rest of the diners.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Nice place to have a simple dinner with friends. Love the food, dessert n beer. Miso salmon grain bowl, fish & chips, Tuscan Seafood Bowl & chicken wings were what we ordered. All were yummy! Dessert - carrot cake n lemon meringue tart : reduced sugar would b better. Overall, nice place!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Visited in the late morning before the weather got too hot , historical park with heritage buildings & a gallery of history of this place was event fully refreshing .

Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Visited in the late morning before the weather got too hot , historical park with heritage buildings & a gallery of history of this place was event fully refreshing .

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Old Airport Road Food Centre exceeded my expectations with a great variety of Singapore heritage dishes. I also loved the creativity behind many stall names referencing places like Chong Pang, Kallang, and Blanco Court — adds local charm.

• Old Airport Road Fried Hokkien Mee (#01-99) – 4/5: Wet-style Hokkien Mee with decent wok hei, generous prawns, good value.

• Famous Old Airport Fried Oyster (#01-101) – 3/5: Soggy omelette, overcooked prawns, not worth $10.

• Chong Pang Huat BBQ (#01-90) – 3/5: Expensive, small portions, hygiene issues.

• Jin Fried Rice (#01-63) – 5/5: Fragrant mala fried rice with pork chop — excellent, must-try.

• Chuan Loo Bee Hokkien Food (#01-111) – 2/5: Tough pork ribs, odd sauce, disappointing.

• Whampoa Soya Bean (#01-68) – 4/5: Solid traditional desserts; soya drinks slightly diluted.

• Unkai Japanese Cuisine (#01-88) – 5/5: Generous salmon bowl, wallet-friendly, satisfying.

• Western Barbeque (#01-53) – 3/5: Chicken chop okay but lacks flavour; queues are long.

• Famous Kallang Airport Fried Oyster (#01-55) – 2/5: Oily, overwhelming, still hunting for a better version.

• Salt (#01-128) – 4/5: Aglio Olio with teriyaki chicken — decent but slightly pricey.

• Yi Ji Fried Prawn Noodles (#01-73) – 3.5/5: Carrot cake decent, noodles not outstanding.

• Xin Mei Xiang Lor Mee (#01-116) – 4/5: Double Fish Lor Mee decent; broth mediocre, some service issues.

• Heritage Tanglin Puff – 5/5: Singapore institution since 1952. Freshly fried puffs with generous fillings — curry potato and curry chicken are standouts. 6 for $12, great value.

• Ah Yee Hong Kong Roasted (#01-77) – 4/5: Three-meat combo rice ($10) — char siew well caramelised, roast pork had satisfying crackling, but roast duck was chewy and rice was dry. Chilli a nice touch. Would love a chicken option on the menu. Worth a visit for the char siew and roast pork.

Overall, a must-visit for variety and heritage flavours, though some stalls are hit-or-miss. Will keep exploring and updating!​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Old Airport Road Food Center is one of popular places for breakfast and lunch.  There are many nice food around such as Lor Mee, Hokkien Mee, Prawn Noddle, Chicken Rice, Roasted Pork and Duck, Western Food, Wanton Noddle.  There is also a new NTUC at level 2.  This food Center can be quite packed during peaked hours.  If you are around Kallang area, I will recommend you guys to drop by …

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Great hawker centre but it can get a little stuffy. Try to grab one of the round tables by the entrances or near the ventilation areas for more space and cool air.

Loved the lor mee! Char koay teow and prawn mee we’re decent too.

If you’re getting ais kacang or cendol, get it from the popular stall in the middle to avoid disappointment!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Very nice and patient uncle who runs this stall. I always thought sugar canes all tasted the same, until I tried this. Happened to find a seat in front of this stall and decided to just buy from him. The sugar cane was really fresh and had a clean taste (there was not that sugary aftertaste). Uncle was also telling me that his is not soaked in sugar syrup and yet it was still naturally sweet! Highly recommended!

I think $2 for a cup, extra for lemon.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: One of my favourite food centres. Great selection and food. Easy to get to via Bus 36 from Changi. Due for a nice power cleaning though.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: My review is mainly on the hawker centre in the community club. This is one place where you can find reasonably priced decent local food such as Malay, Indian and "Chinese" food such as bak chor mee. You can expect to pay about S$7 for a main course and a drink. The place has plenty of fans so it's actually quite a nice place to sit down and enjoy a cup of coffee. For obvious reasons, it does get crowded during lunch hours but during off peak hours, it's a good place to chill.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Quiet in the morning. It was a Tuesday. Many food stalls at the hawker centre were closed. Food lacks variety. It is disappointing that the food vendors  inside the hawker centre do not accept CDC vouchers. The community club does not offer a variety of courses to cater to the community. Mainly exercise courses like zumba and yoga.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Management please look at your dishwashing crew. After washing, both cutlery like metal spoons and fork are super oily.

Also, they don't wash it properly. Many a time I have found bits of leftover curry and other stains on supposedly clean utensils.

Do better.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
The place has a big hawker center with wide variety of food choices at reasonable prices. Ample sitting space and car parking space.

Good Oyster Omelette, Wonton Noodles and Handmade noodles.

There are also a few small eateries on the first floor.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: The hawker centre is quite clean and well-maintained, which is a plus. However, not all the stalls are open. I have visited three times recently, and each time many stalls were closed. It was disappointing not being able to eat what I wanted. On the bright side, it’s not crowded and seats are easy to find. Overall, it’s comfortable to dine in, but the limited number of open stalls affects the experience.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Refurbished recently. Some nice deco outside but not much has changed.
The net playground is still there, which is a good thing and a unique attraction to this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Star Learners @ Hougang Central | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
I have been a parent with Star Learners @ Hougang Central for the past 4 years, and my child has grown up in the centre from a very young age. Over these years, I have witnessed changes within the school, from changes in principals to changes in teachers. As parents, I think it's natural that we don't particularly like staff turnover because we value stability and familiar faces for our children.

However, over time, I've come to realise that change is something we cannot always avoid. What matters more is how the centre manages these changes and continues to provide a positive environment for the children.

One thing I have consistently observed is the steady leadership of Ms Audrey. Since late 2023, she has done a commendable job bringing the team together, maintaining a positive culture, and ensuring that the quality of education and care remains consistent despite the changes.

I would also like to thank the wonderful team of teachers and staff – Zheng Laoshi, Teacher Eunice, Teacher Wiyah, Xiao Yu Laoshi, Teacher Sue, Yi Ling Laoshi, Teacher Aliyyah, Nana Laoshi, Teacher Titus, Liu Laoshi, Auntie Xiao Wei, Auntie Yong and Teacher Juliana. While not all of them have taught my child directly, we interact with many of them during morning drop-offs and afternoon dismissals.

These daily interactions may seem small, but they leave an impression. The teachers are always warm, approachable and welcoming, and they make the effort to greet the children and parents. It gives me confidence knowing that the entire team knows the children and genuinely cares for their well-being.

Of course, there are teachers who have taught my child directly and have played an important role in my child's growth. But what stands out to me is that the care and positivity extend beyond the classroom teachers. There is a strong sense of teamwork amongst the staff, and it creates a nurturing environment for the children.

Over the years, I have seen my child grow in confidence, independence and social skills. The teachers have not only supported my child's learning but have also guided character development and encouraged positive values. As a parent, that is something I truly appreciate.

Thank you to Ms Audrey and the entire Star Learners (Hougang Central) team for your dedication, patience and care. It has been a meaningful 4-year journey for our family, and we are grateful for the love and support that our child has received throughout the years.

Thank you for all that you do. Please know that there are parents like us who genuinely appreciate your efforts, and we will always be behind you, supporting and cheering the team on.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Star Learners @ Hougang Central | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
My child has been with Star Learners @ Hougang Central since 2023. Since the change in leadership, my wife and I have noticed a significant transformation in the centre’s culture and the range of activities offered.

For example, there are now many more field trips than before. The windows are open and the blinds are up, as parents, we truly appreciate the transparency, as we are able to walk by and look in. Previously, the windows were closed and we were unable to see what was happening inside.

Ms Audrey stepped in at the end of 2023 and has remarkably turned the centre around. As parents, we communicate with other families, and all of us have witnessed the positive and meaningful changes she has brought to the centre. Together with her team of teachers, she has cultivated an environment that is positive, authentic and genuine.

My wife and I are very grateful to her and to the wonderful team. We highly recommend Star Learners @ Hougang Central to parents who are considering enrolment. Since Ms Audrey took over, the centre has consistently been full, with a waiting list in place, so do not hesitate.

Thank you to Star Learners @ Hougang Central for the heart and dedication you have shown in caring for the children.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Star Learners @ Hougang Central | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
The school provides a safe, friendly, and nurturing environment where children feel comfortable and happy. We truly appreciate the efforts of the teachers in making learning fun and meaningful for the kids.

Thank you to the entire school team for taking such good care of our daughter and helping her grow with love and confidence. We highly recommend this school to other parents.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Star Learners @ Hougang Central | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
As the year ends we'd like to take a moment to express our gratitude to Principal Audrey and all the teachers and staff at Star Learners Hougang Central, for being such great partners in educating and caring for our little one.

Our child joined PG at 18M (shout out to Teacher Ivy and Ee Leng Laoshi whom she fondly remembers and talks about from time to time) and progressed to N1 this year (shout out to Teacher Joana and Shi Min Laoshi, whom she's formed a strong bond with) and we saw such development since then! We really appreciate that Principal Audrey and her teachers are approachable and update on her progress even outside of the routine term reports.

Lastly, special shout out to Aunty Siow Wei for her gentleness and care for the little ones especially those who have a harder time during drop off (our girl used to be one of them but now she's ok!). Thank you Star Learners Hougang Central team for your dedication in all that you do!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Star Learners @ Hougang Central | Preschool & Childcare
review_text: I wanted to take a moment to express my sincere appreciation for the exceptional learning environment your school provides. The dedication of the teachers, the well-rounded curriculum, and the emphasis on fostering both academic growth and personal development have made a significant positive impact on my child What stands out most is the genuine care and enthusiasm educators bring to the classroom. Lessons are creative, engaging, and tailored to meet diverse learning needs, while open communication between staff and families ensures everyone feels supported. The school's commitment to inclusivity and values like kindness and resilience truly prepares students not just for academic success, but for life beyond the classroom. Additionally, the range of extracurricular activities, encourages students to explore their passions and build confidence. The facilities are well- maintained, and the emphasis on safety and community creates a welcoming atmosphere for all. Thank you for your tireless efforts in nurturing such a positive and inspiring educational experience. Keep up the incredible work! Warm regards, mother : jasmine Nguyen (ayden ang) K2
